THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: 1. DEFENDANT BROADWAY HOTEL, INC. IS DISMISSED AS A DEFENDANT (BUT NOT AS A NOMINAL DEFENDANT) IF NO SUCH DISMISSAL HAS PREVIOUSLY BEEN FILED; 2)JUDGEMENT IS ENTERED IN FAVOR OF PLAINTIFFS ABDUL AND ZARIN PATEL AND AGAINST DEFENDANT NASIR PATEL ON PLAINTIFFS' DIRECT CAUSES OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY, ELDER FINANCIAL ABUSE, AND VIOLATION OF PENAL CODE SECTION 496 FOR THE TOTAL SUM OF $1,117,693.95, WHICH AMOUNT CONSISTS OF $372,564.65 IN COMPENSATORY DAMAGES AND $745,129.30 IN PUNITIVE DAMAGES; 3. THE FOLLOWING INJUNCTION REMAINS IN PLACE: NASIR PATEL MUST: (I)DEPOSIT ALL CASH, CHECKS, AND OTHER RECEIPTS DERIVED FROM BROADWAY OPERATIONS IN THE BROADWAY HOTEL'S WELLS FARGO ACCOUNT; (II)MAINTAIN ALL RECEIPTS; AND (III)PROPERLY ACCOUNT FOR ALL INCOME AND EXPENSES; 4. IF THE COURT'S AWARD ON ABDUL AND ZARIN PATEL'S DIRECT CLAIM IS FOUND TO BE IN ERROR BY A REVIEWING COURT, THEN THE COURT ORDERS THAT NASIR PATEL MUST DEPOSIT $745,129.30 PLUS INTEREST IN THE BROADWAY HOTEL'S WELLS FARGO ACCOUNT AND, FROM THAT SUM, THAT AT LEAST $152,000 PLUS INTEREST BE PAID TO ABDUL AND ZARIN PATEL TO COMPENSATE THEM FOR UNPAID DRAWS FROM DECEMBER 2020 THROUGH DECEMBER 2023. 5. PLAINTIFFS ABDUL AND ZARIN PATEL ARE THE PREVAILING PARTIES WITH RESPECT TO COSTS AND ATTORNEY'S FEES AND PURSUANT TO THE ATTORNEY FEE-SHIFTING PROVISIONS IN WELFARE AND INSTITUTIONS CODE SECTION 15657.5 AND PENAL CODE SECTION 496 AND, IF AN APPEAL RESULTS IN A REVERSAL OF THIS JUDGMENT WITH REGARD TO THE CLAIMS DISCUSSED IN PARAGRAPH 2, ABOVE AND PARAGRAPH 4, ABOVE, APPLIES, THEN ABDUL AND ZARIN PATEL ARE THE PREVAILING PARTIES WITH REGARD TO THEIR COSTS AND ATTORNEY'S FEES PURSUANT TO PENAL CODE SECTION 496.; 6. PLAINTIFF MAY FILE THE APPROPRIATE COSTS MEMORANDUM AND MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES AFTER MEETING AND CONFERRING WITH NASIR PATEL ABOUT THOSE MATTERS AS REQUIRED BY THE FSOD. THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: 1. DEFENDANT BROADWAY HOTEL, INC. IS DISMISSED AS A DEFENDANT (BUT NOT AS A NOMINAL DEFENDANT) IF NO SUCH DISMISSAL HAS PREVIOUSLY BEEN FILED; 2)JUDGEMENT IS ENTERED IN FAVOR OF PLAINTIFFS ABDUL AND ZARIN PATEL AND AGAINST DEFENDANT NASIR PATEL ON PLAINTIFFS' DIRECT CAUSES OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY, ELDER FINANCIAL ABUSE, AND VIOLATION OF PENAL CODE SECTION 496 FOR THE TOTAL SUM OF $1,117,693.95, WHICH AMOUNT CONSISTS OF $372,564.65 IN COMPENSATORY DAMAGES AND $745,129.30 IN PUNITIVE DAMAGES; 3. THE FOLLOWING INJUNCTION REMAINS IN PLACE: NASIR PATEL MUST: (I)DEPOSIT ALL CASH, CHECKS, AND OTHER RECEIPTS DERIVED FROM BROADWAY OPERATIONS IN THE BROADWAY HOTEL'S WELLS FARGO ACCOUNT; (II)MAINTAIN ALL RECEIPTS; AND (III)PROPERLY ACCOUNT FOR ALL INCOME AND EXPENSES; 4. IF THE COURT'S AWARD ON ABDUL AND ZARIN PATEL'S DIRECT CLAIM IS FOUND TO BE IN ERROR BY A REVIEWING COURT, THEN THE COURT ORDERS THAT NASIR PATEL MUST DEPOSIT $745,129.30 PLUS INTEREST IN THE BROADWAY HOTEL'S WELLS FARGO ACCOUNT AND, FROM THAT SUM, THAT AT LEAST $152,000 PLUS INTEREST BE PAID TO ABDUL AND ZARIN PATEL TO COMPENSATE THEM FOR UNPAID DRAWS FROM DECEMBER 2020 THROUGH DECEMBER 2023. 5. PLAINTIFFS ABDUL AND ZARIN PATEL ARE THE PREVAILING PARTIES WITH RESPECT TO COSTS AND ATTORNEY'S FEES AND PURSUANT TO THE ATTORNEY FEE-SHIFTING PROVISIONS IN WELFARE AND INSTITUTIONS CODE SECTION 15657.5 AND PENAL CODE SECTION 496 AND, IF AN APPEAL RESULTS IN A REVERSAL OF THIS JUDGMENT WITH REGARD TO THE CLAIMS DISCUSSED IN PARAGRAPH 2, ABOVE AND PARAGRAPH 4, ABOVE, APPLIES, THEN ABDUL AND ZARIN PATEL ARE THE PREVAILING PARTIES WITH REGARD TO THEIR COSTS AND ATTORNEY'S FEES PURSUANT TO PENAL CODE SECTION 496.; 6. PLAINTIFF MAY FILE THE APPROPRIATE COSTS MEMORANDUM AND MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES AFTER MEETING AND CONFERRING WITH NASIR PATEL ABOUT THOSE MATTERS AS REQUIRED BY THE FSOD. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Post-Judgment Assignment Order (C.C.P. 708.510)
Off Calendar May-08-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS ABDUL PATEL, AS AN INDIVIDUAL AND TRUSTEE OF THE ABDUL RASHID PATEL AND ZARIN ABDUL RASHID PATEL AB LIVING TRUST, and ZARIN PATEL'S Motion For Post-Judgment Assignment Order (C.C.P. 708.510).

Plaintiff's motion for post-judgment assignment order is denied without prejudice. Plaintiff obtained a judgment against defendant Nasir Patel. (Claassen Decl., Ex. A.) Plaintiff seeks to satisfy that judgment by obtaining rents from 1040 Folsom St. because defendant has an ownership in that property. (Claassen Decl., Exs. C, D.) Abulala, LLC is the master tenant of 1040 Folsom St. and it is unclear what rent, if any, it pays to defendant. (N. Patel Decl., pars. 4-7.) The LLC is an entity separate from defendant and plaintiff has not made a sufficient showing in his moving papers to disregard the legal separation. (Curci Investments, LLC v. Baldwin (2017) 14 Cal.App.5th 214, 220-221.) Plaintiff's opening papers do not demonstrate a basis for reverse piercing of the LLC's veil and plaintiff's attempt to rehabilitate the motion by supplying evidence in support of reverse piercing in the reply is improper. (Valentine v. Plum Healthcare Group, LLC (2019) 37 Cal.App.5th 1076, 1089 ["New evidence is generally not permitted with reply papers."]; Regency Outdoor Advertising, Inc. v. Carolina Lanes, Inc. (1995) 31 Cal.App.4th 1323, 1333 [improper to raise new arguments on reply].)

No party has made a motion to seal. All parties are required to redact financial account numbers (except the last four digits where necessary) from publicly filed papers. (See Rule of Court 1.201(a).) Plaintiff is ordered to present for public filing substitute versions of all papers containing financial account numbers by May 22, 2025, and to supply service copies to defendant.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in complaince with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
